mission.title: traefik: Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects
mission.description: Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects. Requested by 85+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
One thing that confused me a bit when trying to set up a 2.0 test was the verbosity of supporting both http and https but with redirects:

```yml
  whoami:
    image: containous/whoami
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      -

steps.title: Apply the fix for Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects
steps.description:
I would like to submit a proposal that is similar to this, but slightly different solution.  If it's too different let me know and I will open a new issue.

I propose that we add **router options to the entrypoints** that will act as the default router options, unless they are specified by the
```yaml
whoami:
    image: containous/whoami
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.middlewares=file.https-redirect"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.tls=true"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I would like to submit a proposal that is similar to this, but slightly different solution.  If it's too different let me know and I will open a new issue.

I propose that we add **router options to the entrypoints** that will act as the default router options, unless they are specified by the router in which case those take precedence.
codeSnippets:
It can't merge arrays, but it's a start.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
